rulings.Case Title: ANDREW PETERSON et al VS. ALFONSO CASTRO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For An Order Deeming Req For Admissions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, September 24, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFFs ANDREW PETERSON & SUSAN PETERSON's Motion For An Order Deeming Request For Admissions. Granted in part and denied in part. Pursuant to CCP sec. 2033.280, Requests for Admission nos. 1-7 and 20-38 are deemed admitted. The motion is denied as to Requests for Admission nos. 8-19. Defendant Zach Georgopoulous timely objected to these RFAs, asserting attorney-client privilege on behalf of his client. (See Union Bank of California, N.A. v. Superior Court (2005) 130 Cal.App.4th 378, 399, "[I]n the case of the attorney-client privilege, although the client is the holder of the privilege, the attorney must claim the privilege unless instructed otherwise by a person with the power to waive the privilege.") Further, Plaintiffs cite no authority that supports their contention that the attorney-client privilege may be waived by deeming Requests for Admissions admitted. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL BOREEN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn Of Petition For Administrative Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, September 24, 2014, Line 2, PETITIONER DANIEL BOREEN's Petition For Administrative Mandamus. The petition is denied. On September 8, 2014, the court ordered petitioner to provide the court with a written copy of the administrative record. The court warned petitioner that his failure to comply with that order may result in denial of his petition. The petitioner failed to comply with the court's order. It is petitioner's responsibility to provide the court with the record. See Foster v. Civil Service Commission (1983) 142 Cal.App.3d 444, 453. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE RALLO-ECKHOFF et al VS. ARCON CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To: Supplemental Interrogatories (Set 1); 2) Special Interrogatories 3) Req. For Admissions (Set 1) And For Sanction Against Arcon Construction Co., Inc. And Its Attorneys Of Record, Jointly And Severally
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 24, 2014, Line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT LIAS MECHANICAL INC. Motion To Compel Further Responses To: Supplemental Interrogatories (Set 1); 2) Special Interrogatories 3) Request For Admissions (Set 1) And For Sanction Against Arcon Construction Co., Inc. And Its Attorneys Of Record, Jointly And Severally. Pro Tem Judge Vedica Puri, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion as to Supp. Interrogatories, Set 1 and Special Interrogatories, Set 2 is GRANTED IN PART. Further responses to Supp. Interrogatory Nos. 3 and 4 setting forth a general damages range and how those damages were calculated, in general, shall be served 15 days after notice of entry of this order. The motion is DENIED with respect to Supp. Interrogatory No. 2. Further responses to Special Interrogatory No. 7 setting forth, in general, what defects are alleged as against moving party, shall be served 15 days after notice of entry of this order. Further response to Special Interrogatory No. 9, which contains objections only, shall be served 15 days after notice of entry of this order. The motion is DENIED with respect to Special Interrogatory Nos. 6 and 12 and as to the request for a privilege log at this time. The parties agree the motion is moot as to RFAs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to vpuri@plmlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: S.F. GLOBAL SOURCING LLC VS. GP DISTRIBUTION GROUP INC., A SUSPENDED CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings And Court Order Releasing Uncontested Interpled Funds
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, September 24, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT T&G 2009 LLC's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings And Court Order Releasing Uncontested Interpled Funds. The motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. Defendant's moving papers argue as the sole basis for judgment that all of the other defendants have defaulted. Defendant fails to show an entitlement to judgment because paragraphs 8-10, 13, and the prayer for relief of the complaint dispute defendant T&G's entitlement to the funds. The court takes those allegations as true when reviewing this motion. Defendant's moving papers do not sufficiently raise the issue regarding the propriety of this interpleader action because plaintiff claims an interest in the res. At any rate, City of Morgan Hill v. Brown (1999) 71 Cal.App.4th 1114, 1122, indicates that this action may proceed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GIGI ELLIS VS. JUNYING YU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Junying Yu'S Motion For Summary Judgment Per The Order Of The Presiding Judge.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, September 24, 2014, Line 13, Defendant Junying Yu'S Motion For Summary Judgment Per The Order Of The Presiding Judge. Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Triable issues of material fact exist as to whether the nonjudicial foreclosure sale and subsequent deed of trust executed by J.P. Morgan Chase lawfully divested Plaintiff of title in the property. (See Plaintiff's Proposed Amended Responsive Separate Statement 4, 5.) Defendant has failed to present evidence controverting Plaintiff's claim to title as a matter of law. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GIGI ELLIS VS. JUNYING YU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Gigi Ellis' Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, September 24, 2014, Line 13, Plaintiff Gigi Ellis' Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Motion denied. The Answer as pled states sufficient facts to constitute a defense. Defendant sufficiently denied Plaintiff's ownership in the property and alleged, by admitting facts pled in the Complaint, Defendant's ownership in the property. Furthermore, judicial notice of the recorded documents does not establish that Deutsche Bank was not the beneficiary at the time of the foreclosure sale or that Defendant does not have title to the property. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
